elements.sent-2411-006: Superconformal gauge theories have proven to be a valuable playground for refining our understanding of non-perturbative effects in gauge theories and in holography.
elements.sent-2411-007: Following two ground-breaking results in the context of string theory, namely the description of the microstates of certain asymptotically flat supersymmetric black hole as brane excitations~\cite{Strominger:1996sh and the development of AdS/CFT~\cite{Maldacena:1997re , it was naturally proposed that AdS black hole microstates should be understood as gauge-invariant states in a dual superconformal gauge theory~\cite{Witten:1998zw .}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: In the supersymmetric context, the hope was to make this correspondence between bulk microstates and gauge-invariant boundary states precise, see e.g.~\cite{Kinney:2005ej,Berkooz:2006wc,Kim:2006he,Janik:2007pm,Grant:2008sk,Chang:2013fba for notable early attempts.}

elements.sent-2411-350: Given a superconformal index $\mathcal{I}\,$, we have shown that there are infinitely many possible Bethe expansions associated to it, with some of them being more convenient to use than others.
elements.sent-2411-351: All of them compute $\mathcal{I}$ as a finite sum over solutions to Bethe equations.
elements.sent-2411-352: Our formalism applies to general superconformal theories, and maybe even beyond.\footnote{ Technically, it applies to any integral of the form~\eqref{eq:TheIntegral with a meromorphic integrand~$I$ of the form~\eqref{eq:IntegrandProd that obeys properties~\ref{property1 ,~\ref{property2 , and~\ref{property3 .}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-353: Using this formalism, we have proposed solutions to two main problems:.
elements.sent-2411-354: Find all the Bethe solutions that (potentially) contribute to the superconformal index.
elements.sent-2411-355: Evaluate the contribution to the index from the continuous solutions.
elements.sent-2411-356: We have already given a summary of general results in Section~\ref{sec1 .}
elements.sent-2411-357: In this final section we focus on summarizing the salient points for the concrete example of $U(N)$ $\mathcal{N =4$ SYM with $p=q\,$.}
elements.sent-2411-358: First, let us summarize our results for problem (1).
elements.sent-2411-359: Topological solutions are distinguished by their behavior under cyclic symmetries.
elements.sent-2411-360: For example, the irreducible topological solutions are representations of $\mathbb{Z _N\,$, the center symmetry within the Weyl symmetry group of~$U(N)$ $\mathcal{N =4$ SYM.}}
elements.sent-2411-361: On the other hand, the reducible topological solutions are made of representations of smaller cyclic groups $\mathbb{Z _{N'}$ with $N'<N$.}
elements.sent-2411-362: Non-topological solutions can not be determined using cyclic symmetries.
elements.sent-2411-363: To compute these we use trigonometric-like expansions near $q=0$ under which the Bethe equations simplify.
elements.sent-2411-364: In fact, these expansions systematically determine all solutions, both topological and non-topological.
elements.sent-2411-365: These expansions are fixed by certain boundary conditions which are determined by the set of all possible asymptotic expansions of both topological and non-topological solutions near $q=0\,$.
elements.sent-2411-366: We claim that topological and non-topological solutions share the same set of leading asymptotic behaviors near $q=0$.\footnote{Initially, we were led to explore this possibility because tachyonic contributions coming from topological solutions in the expansion around $q=0\,$, needed to be cancelled by tachyonic contributions coming from non-topological solutions in the very same expansion.}
elements.sent-2411-367: Thus, one can first determine all the topological solutions using cyclic symmetries, then use them to fix all the trigonometric-like expansions, and finally use these expansions to obtain all the non-topological solutions in perturbations around $q=0\,$.
elements.sent-2411-368: Under these trigonometric-like expansions, the Bethe equations become rational in fugacities, and the topological solutions are determined by $N$ zeroes of a finite number of order-$N$ polynomials in a single complex variable.
elements.sent-2411-369: The Galois group of one generic such polynomial is the symmetric group $S_N\,$.
elements.sent-2411-370: The breaking of such Galois group indicates the emergence of a spontaneously broken discrete symmetry that exchanges the corresponding $N$ roots among themselves,\footnote{ This is $\mathbb{Z _N$ one-form center symmetry identified in~\cite{Cabo-Bizet:2021jar .}}}
elements.sent-2411-371: For contributing non-topological solutions, the Galois group of the corresponding order-$N$ polynomial remains unbroken.
elements.sent-2411-372: In particular, the $\mathbb{Z _{N}$ symmetry is explicitly broken by these solutions.}
elements.sent-2411-373: This can be immediately seen from their positions: in contrast to the topological solutions, the positions of non-topological ones depend on the flavour rapidities, e.g., on $Y_{1}\,$.
elements.sent-2411-374: For generic superconformal indices, we expect the relevant trigonometric-like expansions to be fixed by the balancing condition~\eqref{eq:BalancingCondition .}
elements.sent-2411-375: However, further study is needed to put this conclusion on firm ground.
elements.sent-2411-376: Regarding problem (2), we have shown that the contribution of non-topological solutions to the index localizes to isolated points in $\gamma\,$.
elements.sent-2411-377: The details of this localization process have been illustrated in the concrete example of $N=3\,$.
elements.sent-2411-378: First of all, continuous solutions $\gamma$ intersect with the reducible topological solutions.
elements.sent-2411-379: We have shown that the reducible topological solutions do not contribute to the index.
elements.sent-2411-380: The reason is that the contribution of $\gamma$'s gets localized to isolated points for which there is no cyclic-symmetry enhancement, whereas the reducible topological solutions still obey some cyclic symmetry.
elements.sent-2411-381: Then for $N=3$, we have checked that the tachyonic contributions coming from the irreducible topological solutions are cancelled by tachyonic contributions from the non-topological solutions, in a rather non-trivial way that involves cancellations among several large rational coefficients of order $10^{8}\,$.
elements.sent-2411-382: We expect these cancellations to continue to hold for generic values of $N\,$.
elements.sent-2411-383: This is in a sense predicted by the generality of the rather simple residue identity~\eqref{eq:ResFormula , which relies solely on the meromorphicity of $I\,$ and the $t=q$-periodicity property~\eqref{eq:PeriodicityQOper .}}
elements.sent-2411-384: Finally, we mention some interesting future directions:.
elements.sent-2411-385: It would be interesting to apply our formalism to study systems beyond the 4D superconformal indices.
elements.sent-2411-386: For example, our methods can be applied to study four-dimensional superconformal indices in the presence of superconformal defects~\cite{Chen:2023lzq,Cabo-Bizet:2023ejm,Amariti:2024bsr .}
elements.sent-2411-387: They can be also applied to study four-dimensional topologically twisted indices~\cite{Hong:2018viz .}
elements.sent-2411-388: Instead of the approach we have followed in this paper, Bethe expansions can be also derived using non-abelian equivariant localization formulae.
elements.sent-2411-389: The details of such derivation will be reported in version 2 of~\cite{Cabo-Bizet:2020ewf .}
elements.sent-2411-390: For $\mathcal{N =4$ SYM, it would be nice to reproduce the index up to higher orders in powers of $q$ and for higher values of~$N$.}
elements.sent-2411-391: In particular, we would like to determine the large-$N$ behavior of the continuous solutions and to understand their gravitational/stringy bulk duals.
elements.sent-2411-392: Also for $\mathcal{N =4$ SYM, we have presented a simpler Bethe expansion for generic cases $p\neq q$,~\eqref{eq:QPQOperator .}}
elements.sent-2411-393: We expect that this expansion, together with the proposed methods, will lead to the resolution of the puzzles recently raised in~\cite{Aharony:2024ntg .}
